projects.name: Android x86-64 PC Game Compatibility
projects.notes: Solo owner. Qualcomm vs InnoPeak benchmark pending — update when ready. Steam launcher is a possible future product detail; omit from public resume until disclosure is clear.

bullets.text: Led end-to-end architecture and delivery of x86-64 PC game compatibility on Android, by composing chroot Ubuntu, ARM64EC Wine, FEX-EMU, and DXVK for DirectX-to-Vulkan on mobile GPUs.
Improved frame rate and power draw 5–10% versus Winlator on Snapdragon 8 Elite, by tuning the x86 stack for stable frame pacing on Android.
Validated AAA PC title compatibility on Android including The Witcher 3, by systematic compatibility and performance testing toward broader library coverage.
Designed a consumer Android x86 gaming product with one-tap setup versus manual Winlator workflows, by platform chroot integration and benchmarking against Qualcomm reference builds.

projects.name: Unified Rendering
projects.notes: Contributor (not sole owner); supported unified rendering when capacity allowed, Nov 2025–Jan 2026. Feature shipped on a subset of production devices; rollout scaling as bug fixes land. Team now in maintenance/bug-fix phase. Various bug fixes across the initiative.

bullets.text: Contributed to AOSP unified rendering shipping on production devices, consolidating UI into one SurfaceFlinger renderer context, by replacing per-app buffer queues with display-list submission.
Centralized GPU allocation for textures, buffers, shaders, and typefaces in unified rendering, by Binder and shared-memory IPC with serialized payloads and stable client-side handles.
Reduced frame hitching from ~30% to under 1% during GPU resource sync, by fixing race conditions between client apps and the centralized renderer.

projects.name: Layer Caching
projects.notes: Primary technical driver; started solo, onboarded two engineers via shared template. No overlap with Unified Rendering (started Nov 2025 when this ended). Shipped on all company production devices. Patent may list user as inventor but was authored elsewhere — confirm before citing on resumes.

bullets.text: Led AOSP layer caching to all company production devices with 12% lower power during high-frequency UI interactions, by owning architecture and onboarding two engineers via a reusable template.
Designed a reusable SurfaceFlinger layer-caching template for expensive UI effects, enabling blur, shadows, and rounded corners on independent cache lifetimes without pipeline re-architecture.
Restored hardware-composer eligibility for cached UI layers, by dirty-region invalidation in RenderEngine that repaints only overlay-affected regions during blur and shadows.
Kept cached layers on hardware-composer compose paths across Qualcomm and MediaTek SoCs, by diagnosing composition fallbacks using vendor HWC logs and per-platform layer budgets.

projects.name: Variable Rate Shading
projects.notes: Solo owner end-to-end; preceded Layer Caching (Jul 2024). Deep R&D into reduced fragment shading—API VRS (OpenGL/Vulkan), checkerboard, foveated rendering, custom stencil-buffer VRS, and image reconstruction/infill for undersampled regions. Only API-backed VRS shipped on all company production devices; checkerboard and foveated not deployed due to quality/vendor concerns. Custom stencil approach explored but lacked meaningful performance gains—not pursued. Follow-on to OpenGL interception (ended Jun 2022): replaced texture-based per-texture shading rates with frame-based API VRS (mode 2). Inventor on three VRS-related patents; patent numbers TBD. Delivered ~90-min internal presentation to ~30 engineers; authored extensive internal documentation.

bullets.text: Sole owner of variable-rate shading R&D as inventor on three related patents, shipping API VRS on all company devices with ~10% rendering gains on flagship hardware.
Raised ray-tracing demo frame rates from ~25 to 60–65 FPS on heavy scenes and ~40 to 95 FPS on lighter workloads, by integrating Variable Rate Shading into the pipeline.
Improved Unity rendering performance ~7% with a Vulkan-intercepted VRS plugin, by pre-generating shading-rate attachments at instance and device creation before context init.
Shipped frame-based API Variable Rate Shading on production devices with ~7–10% rendering gains, by replacing texture-based per-texture shading in the OpenGL ES interception layer.
Presented VRS research at Qualcomm headquarters and collaborated with Android OS and GPU vendors, deploying optimizations across millions of consumer devices with production VRS rollout.

projects.name: OpenGL Game Interception
projects.notes: Graphics engineer on ~5-person team (3 graphics, 1–2 system). System engineers owned interception layer and package-to-module routing; graphics owned per-game modules. User owned PUBG and Honor of Kings; also helped shape which OpenGL functions and per-call data the team analyzed. ~7–9 flagship titles (LoL, PUBG, Honor of Kings, story games, Chinese-market racing title — name TBD, possibly QQ Speed). Texture-based API shading rates here; frame-based VRS succeeded this in proj-innopeak-vrs (Jul 2022+). Related patents on interception structure and game-mode detection via API analysis may exist — user not listed as inventor; confirm titles/numbers before citing.

bullets.text: Contributed to OpenGL ES game interception still used five years after release, by defining per-call analysis APIs and graphics signals for frame structure, game mode, and texture-based shading rates.
Owned per-title optimization modules for PUBG and Honor of Kings on the shared platform, by reverse-engineering OpenGL call sequences and frame patterns for state-specific GPU tuning.
Cut GPU power ~5% on flagship game workloads, by offline texture edge-frequency analysis and runtime hash lookup assigning per-texture API shading rates from 1×1 through 4×4.
Delivered individualized per-game optimization on a shared interception framework, by partnering with system engineers on package detection and dispatch into implementation modules.

projects.name: Native Android Testbed Applications
projects.notes: First project after hire; preceded OpenGL Game Interception (Aug 2020). Contributor across multiple org teams—not sole owner of any single testbed. Prior ~1 year of native Android and Java app development before joining InnoPeak. Camera work used Camera2 API for multi-camera HDR testing; team cared about preserving capture fidelity but user does not recall specific techniques—keep resume bullets factual, not speculative.

bullets.text: Built native Android Java testbeds for multiple org teams, by optimizing runtime performance so larger test suites ran within the same hardware constraints.
Built Camera2 multi-camera HDR capture testbed workflows on company devices, by implementing capture and processing paths for org-wide sensor validation.
Configured sensor testbed workflows on company devices, by wiring applications to read device sensors and validate incoming sensor data streams.
Accelerated prototyping and boilerplate generation using AI-assisted development tools, by keeping architectural oversight through structured code review on generated changes.
